name: Zodios (Zod-Powered TypeScript API Client)

auth.notes: HTTP client library; auth via custom plugins

performance.notes: axios-based; Zod validation overhead minimal; runtime type checking adds ~1-2ms

agent_readiness.notes: End-to-end typesafe HTTP client using Zod schemas. Define API contract with makeApi([{method, path, parameters, response}]). Client validates both request and response at runtime via Zod. Express/Fastify server adapters share schema between client and server. @zodios/express, @zodios/react for react-query integration. Immutable API definition. Alternative: ts-rest is similar but uses tRPC-style contracts. openapi-ts is type-only without runtime validation.

gotchas: Built on axios (not native fetch — heavier than ky/got)
Less popular than trpc for internal APIs
best_when: REST APIs needing both compile-time types and runtime validation
Client-server TypeScript apps sharing API contracts via Zod
Teams wanting Zod validation on API responses (not just types)
Projects using axios wanting type-safe HTTP
avoid_when: Simple fetch with no runtime validation needed
summary: Zodios is an end-to-end typesafe HTTP client using Zod schemas for both compile-time types and runtime validation. Define API contracts once, validate requests and responses. Express/Fastify server adapters share schemas client-to-server. Best for TypeScript REST APIs where runtime response validation matters alongside type safety. tRPC is simpler for internal TypeScript-to-TypeScript APIs.
